mem-005-img-f1b7a3.query: I was a scribe in Alexandria. I watched the smoke rise for three days. We saved what we could — I carried scrolls under my robe until my skin blistered. Some knowledge was lost forever. The Library That Burned

mem-007-img-a9f4b1.query: The war had taken everything except us. We married in the shell of the church, no roof, stars visible above. My mother sang. The soldiers on both sides stopped shooting for an hour. The Wedding in the Ruins

mem-010-img-b3c9d5.query: The water was cold and fast. My father held my hand so tight I thought my fingers would break. On the other side, he knelt and kissed the ground. I did not understand then. I do now. Crossing the Rio Grande

mem-014-img-c2d5e9.query: Three thousand of us on the shore. The elders performed the haka one last time on Earth soil. The sound rose into the sky. I felt it in my chest, in my bones, in something older than memory. Maori Haka Before the Voyage

mem-013-img-a8b4c7.query: We typed "LOGIN" and the system crashed after "LO." But those two letters traveled forty miles through a wire. We called the other lab and said: did you get it? They said: we got LO. We laughed for an hour. The First Internet Message

mem-012-img-d1e6c4.query: She never wrote it down. I watched her hands for forty years, trying to memorize every pinch of spice, every fold of dough. When she died, I cooked it from memory. It was almost right. Almost. Grandmother's Recipe

mem-015-img-e4f1a6.query: I was twelve when the first locomotive came through our village. The horses bolted. The women screamed. The men stood very still. Then my father laughed — a laugh I had never heard from him before. The Steam Engine Roars

mem-011-img-f5a2b8.query: I set the type myself, letter by letter. When the first page came off the press, I held it to the light. Words that had taken a monk a year to copy — done in an hour. The world would never be the same. The Printing Press Speaks

mem-016-img-b7c3d1.query: The forest at night is not silent. It breathes. It hums. I lay in my hammock and listened to ten thousand species living their lives around me, indifferent to my presence, magnificent in their indifference. Midnight in the Amazon
